title: GitLab CI Job Artifact Upload Failures by Project
dataSources: `index=devops` GitLab CI pipeline JSON (`sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline`) from GitLab webhooks or Sidekiq export
app: GitLab Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 6848) or GitLab webhook → HEC
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:pipeline" status=failed
| search "uploading artifacts" OR "Uploading artifacts" OR artifact*
| stats count as failures, values(job_name) as jobs by project_id, project_path
| where failures >= 3
| sort -failures
description: Detects recurring artifact upload failures that block downstream deploy stages and retention policies.
value: Surfaces projects where artifact publishing breaks so release artifacts and security scans stay available.
implementation: Enable pipeline webhooks or use the GitLab Add-on; ensure `job_name`, `status`, and failure messages are extracted. Alert when failures repeat for one project in 24h.
visualization: Table (projects with failure counts), Bar chart (failures by project).
knownFalsePositives: Activity shifts during holidays, release freezes, or vendor maintenance; confirm against your change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We watch GitLab runner and pipeline signals so broken jobs, storage, or runners do not stall your teams.
